rag_rules: Preserve structural ownership: place behavior only in the scope that can legally see the referenced names.
Keep type-level contracts limited to component features visible at that type; do not rely on implementation-only paths there.
Use implementation scope only for implementation-owned bindings after the relevant structural elements exist.
When scope errors arise, move only the offending implementation-specific content rather than restructuring the architecture.
generic_rules: Do not change component hierarchy, features, connections, or properties when inserting behavior.
Attach new content to the owning declaration and preserve surrounding AADL structure and ordering.
Prefer the narrowest valid owner scope that makes all referenced identifiers visible.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Component declarations and implementation declarations have different visible namespaces; references must match the chosen owner scope.
Subcomponent or connection-derived names are not valid in a type-owned contract unless exposed through valid features or variables.
anti_patterns: Do not insert content into a scope that cannot legally reference its identifiers.
Do not move, rename, or delete architectural elements just to make inserted content fit.
irrelevant_topics: Detailed clause forms for assumptions, guarantees, equations, constants, or assignments are not needed for this fusion step.
Requirement-level output dependency patterns are secondary to owner/scope legality for this agent.
